total_known_works.source: Damien Hirst official catalogues, Gagosian, Newport Street Gallery

axis_weight_schema.note: 각 작품은 5축 가중치 합계 ~1.0~2.0. 단일 축 0.7+ 시 그 축의 대표작.

exhibition_note.ko: Goldsmiths 학생 시절 첫 벽화 스팟. 격자 구조의 출발점.
exhibition_note.en: First wall spots during Goldsmiths years. Genesis of the grid structure.

significance.ko: Spot Paintings 시리즈의 단초. 격자·산업·우연의 원형.
significance.en: Origin of the Spot Paintings series. Archetype of grid, industry, chance.

exhibition_note.ko: 최초의 Medicine Cabinet 중 하나. Freeze 직후 약장 시리즈 시작.
exhibition_note.en: Among the first Medicine Cabinets. Series begins shortly after Freeze.

significance.ko: 약=신앙의 등식이 처음 명시된 작품. 진열장-제단 형식의 출발점.
significance.en: First explicit equation of pharmaceutical = faith. Origin of the vitrine-as-altar form.

exhibition_note.ko: 초기 캔버스 Spot Painting. Hirst 본인이 직접 그린 ~5점 중 하나로 추정.
exhibition_note.en: Early canvas Spot Painting. Believed to be among the ~5 painted by Hirst himself.

significance.ko: 벽화에서 캔버스로의 이행. 산업적 연작 체계의 정착.
significance.en: Transition from wall to canvas. Consolidation of the industrial serial system.

exhibition_note.ko: Sinner 후속. 신체 부위별 약학 분류 — 머리·가슴·복부·다리.
exhibition_note.en: Follow-up to Sinner. Pharmaceutical taxonomy by body region — head, chest, abdomen, legs.

significance.ko: 약학적 분류학을 신체 지도학과 결합한 초기 사례.
significance.en: Early case binding pharmaceutical taxonomy to bodily cartography.

works.title: A Thousand Years
works.medium: glass, steel, MDF, cow head, flies, maggots, sugar, water, Insect-O-Cutor

exhibition_note.ko: Gambler(1990) 출품. 알·구더기·파리·죽음의 닫힌 순환을 진열장에 봉인.
exhibition_note.en: Exhibited at Gambler (1990). Sealed life cycle — egg, maggot, fly, death — within a vitrine.

significance.ko: Hirst 자연사 시리즈의 사실상 출발점. 1991 Shark의 직접적 선행.
significance.en: De facto origin of Hirst's Natural History series. Direct precursor to the 1991 Shark.

exhibition_note.ko: Sex Pistols 곡 제목 인용. 펑크와 약학적 권태의 결합.
exhibition_note.en: Title borrowed from the Sex Pistols. Punk meets pharmaceutical ennui.

significance.ko: 약장의 음악·하위문화 인용. Hirst 도상 어휘의 확장.
significance.en: Cabinet's citation of music and subculture. Expansion of Hirst's iconographic vocabulary.

works.title: The Physical Impossibility of Death in the Mind of Someone Living
works.medium: glass, steel, silicone, formaldehyde, tiger shark

exhibition_note.ko: Saatchi 의뢰 £50,000. 14피트 호주 타이거 샤크. YBA·Hirst의 정의적 작품.
exhibition_note.en: Commissioned by Saatchi for £50,000. 14-foot Australian tiger shark. The defining work of YBA and of Hirst.

significance.ko: 경험으로 죽음을 인식할 수 없음을 제목이 명시. 21c 미술의 분기점.
significance.en: Title explicitly names the impossibility of knowing death through experience. Turning point of 21c art.

works.title: In and Out of Love (White Paintings and Live Butterflies / Black Paintings and Dead Butterflies)
works.medium: household gloss on canvas, live & dead butterflies, two rooms

exhibition_note.ko: Woodstock Street 갤러리. 한 방엔 살아있는 나비, 다른 방엔 캔버스에 박힌 나비.
exhibition_note.en: Woodstock Street Gallery. One room of live butterflies hatching, another of dead butterflies on canvas.

significance.ko: 생/사 이항을 공간으로 분리한 초기 설치. 나비 시리즈 전체의 모태.
significance.en: Early installation separating life/death binary into space. Womb of the entire Butterfly series.

exhibition_note.ko: Hirst 16세 시절 Leeds 영안실 사진. 1991년 에디션화. 자연사 시리즈의 자전적 원시 장면.
exhibition_note.en: Photograph from Leeds morgue when Hirst was 16. Editioned 1991. Autobiographical primal scene of the Natural History series.

significance.ko: 자전적 발원. 죽음과의 첫 시각적 대면이 기록됨.
significance.en: Autobiographical origin. First visual confrontation with death recorded.

exhibition_note.ko: Cohen 갤러리 첫 설치. 1998년 Notting Hill 동명 레스토랑으로 확장. 약학적 우주의 도해.
exhibition_note.en: First installed at Cohen Gallery. Expanded into the eponymous Notting Hill restaurant in 1998. Diagram of the pharmaceutical universe.

auction_record.note: Pharmacy restaurant fixtures sold after closure

significance.ko: 약장 미술의 정점. 약=종교 등식의 공간적 실현.
significance.en: Apex of cabinet art. Spatial realization of pharmaceutical = religion.

works.title: Mother and Child Divided
works.medium: glass, steel, formaldehyde, cow and calf, bisected

exhibition_note.ko: 베니스 비엔날레 Aperto. Premio 2000 수상. 어미 소와 송아지를 양분하여 4개 진열장에 안치.
exhibition_note.en: Venice Biennale Aperto. Won Premio 2000. Cow and calf bisected into four vitrines.

significance.ko: 진열장 사이를 관람자가 통과 — 분리된 모성을 신체로 체험. Hirst의 정서적 정점.
significance.en: Viewer passes between the vitrines — bodily experiences the divided maternity. Emotional zenith of Hirst's oeuvre.

exhibition_note.ko: Pharmaceuticals 부제 시리즈. 작품명은 실제 화학 물질명에서 차용.
exhibition_note.en: Pharmaceuticals subtitle series. Title borrowed from a real chemical compound.

significance.ko: Spot=약명 매핑 규칙의 명시. 의미 거부의 의미를 화학식이 보증.
significance.en: Codifies the Spot = drug-name mapping. The chemical formula warrants the refusal of meaning.

exhibition_note.ko: Serpentine 전시. 1994년 한 학생이 잉크를 부어 훼손되었다가 복원.
exhibition_note.en: Exhibited at Serpentine. Vandalized in 1994 by a student pouring ink, later restored.

significance.ko: 기독교적 양 도상의 포름알데히드화. 길 잃은 양 비유의 직역.
significance.en: Christian sheep iconography put into formaldehyde. Literalization of the lost-sheep parable.

works.title: Loving in a World of Desire
works.medium: vitrine, beach ball suspended on air jet

exhibition_note.ko: 베이지 비치볼이 공기 분사로 진열장 내부에 떠 있다. 욕망의 부유 상태.
exhibition_note.en: Beige beach ball levitated by air jet inside vitrine. The suspended state of desire.

significance.ko: 중력 거부와 욕망 — Koons 'Equilibrium'의 영국적 번역.
significance.en: Gravity refused and desire — a British translation of Koons's Equilibrium.

works.title: Some Comfort Gained from the Acceptance of the Inherent Lies in Everything
works.medium: glass, steel, formaldehyde, two cows, each bisected into multiple sections

exhibition_note.ko: Turner Prize 수상작. 두 마리 소를 각각 6조각으로 양분하여 12개 진열장에 교차 배치.
exhibition_note.en: Turner Prize winner. Two cows each cut into six sections, alternated across 12 vitrines.

significance.ko: 1995 Turner Prize 수상. 진리의 분절 — 전체는 진열장 사이의 빈 공간에 있다.
significance.en: Won 1995 Turner Prize. Truth segmented — the whole resides in the gaps between vitrines.

works.title: This Little Piggy Went to Market, This Little Piggy Stayed at Home
works.medium: glass, steel, formaldehyde, pig (bisected, motorized vitrines)

exhibition_note.ko: 두 진열장이 모터로 좌우 분리·결합. 동요(아동가)가 도살 우화로 변환.
exhibition_note.en: Two motorized vitrines slide apart and reunite. Nursery rhyme converted into a slaughter parable.

significance.ko: 운동성을 진열장에 도입. 절단=시간의 가시화.
significance.en: Introduces motion to the vitrine. Bisection = the visibility of time.

exhibition_note.ko: Gagosian 뉴욕 개인전 표제작. Hirst의 Larry Gagosian과 본격 협업의 시작.
exhibition_note.en: Title work of Hirst's solo show at Gagosian New York. Beginning of full Gagosian partnership.

significance.ko: 약장 시리즈 미국 시장 진입. 미국 비평계의 Hirst 수용 시작.
significance.en: Cabinet series enters the US market. American critical reception begins.

works.title: He Tried to Internalise Everything
works.medium: glass, steel, ashtray, table, chair, cigarettes

exhibition_note.ko: 흡연자의 진열장. 자기 파괴를 일상 가구로 봉인.
exhibition_note.en: Vitrine of a smoker. Self-destruction sealed into ordinary furniture.

significance.ko: 자연사 진열장의 인간화. 일상 = 만성적 죽음.
significance.en: Humanization of the natural-history vitrine. Daily life = chronic death.

works.title: Beautiful, Hypnotic, Holographic, Slowly Spinning Painting
works.medium: household gloss on canvas, spun on rotating apparatus

exhibition_note.ko: Spin Painting 시리즈 초기. 'Beautiful…' 명명 규칙의 정착.
exhibition_note.en: Early Spin Painting. Codifies the "Beautiful…" naming convention.

significance.ko: 원심력=우연=카니발. Pollock 우연성의 산업화.
significance.en: Centrifugal force = chance = carnival. Industrialization of Pollock's chance.

works.title: The Acquired Inability to Escape
works.medium: glass, steel, office furniture, ashtray, lighter

exhibition_note.ko: 사무실 진열장 시리즈. Sensation 전(왕립아카데미) 출품.
exhibition_note.en: Office vitrine series. Exhibited in Sensation at Royal Academy.

significance.ko: Sensation 전(1997)을 통해 YBA의 정전화에 결정적 기여.
significance.en: Decisively contributes to the canonization of YBA via Sensation (1997).

works.title: Hymn
works.medium: painted bronze (originally based on Humbrol Young Scientist Anatomy Set)

exhibition_note.ko: Humbrol 해부학 모형의 6m 거대화. 후일 저작권 합의금 지급.
exhibition_note.en: 6m enlargement of a Humbrol anatomy toy. Hirst later paid a copyright settlement.

significance.ko: 공장 생산 + 신체 도해. 의학과 차용의 결합.
significance.en: Factory production meets bodily diagram. Medicine and appropriation conjoined.

exhibition_note.ko: 13장의 약 포장지 디자인 — 'BEANS CHIPS' 'STEAK + KIDNEY' 등 식·약·성찬 합치.
exhibition_note.en: 13 pharmaceutical packaging designs — "BEANS CHIPS," "STEAK + KIDNEY" — merging food, medicine, and Eucharist.

significance.ko: 최후의 만찬 도상의 약학적 번역. 가톨릭과 약학 시리즈의 봉합점.
significance.en: Pharmaceutical translation of Last Supper iconography. Suture point between Catholic and Pharmaceuticals series.

works.title: Adam and Eve (Banished from the Garden)

exhibition_note.ko: 성서적 추방을 자연사 진열장으로 옮긴 작업.
exhibition_note.en: Biblical expulsion transposed into a natural-history vitrine.

significance.ko: 신학적 서사의 박물학화. FAITH × MORTALITY 결합 정점.
significance.en: Theological narrative naturalized as natural-history. Apex of the FAITH × MORTALITY conjunction.

exhibition_note.ko: 1960년대 영국 자선모금함을 거대화. 소아마비 소녀상이 길거리에 우뚝.
exhibition_note.en: Enlargement of a 1960s British charity collection box. The polio-girl figure stands monumental in the street.

significance.ko: 공공조각으로의 진출. 시민종교적 모성과 연민의 도상학.
significance.en: Entry into public sculpture. Iconography of civic-religious maternity and compassion.

exhibition_note.ko: 삼위일체 도그마를 의학 3분과로 번역. 약학기 후기 대표작.
exhibition_note.en: Trinity dogma translated into three medical disciplines. Late representative work of the pharmaceutical period.

significance.ko: 신학 어휘의 의학적 전유. 약장-제단 형식의 신학적 정점.
significance.en: Theological vocabulary appropriated for medicine. Theological apex of the cabinet-altar form.

works.title: Doorways to the Kingdom of Heaven
works.medium: butterflies and household gloss on canvas

exhibition_note.ko: Butterfly Kaleidoscope 회화의 출발 시기. 고딕 장미창의 21c 재기입.
exhibition_note.en: Origin moment of the Butterfly Kaleidoscope paintings. 21c re-inscription of the Gothic rose window.

significance.ko: 나비=스테인드글라스 등식의 명문화. 신앙기의 시각적 핵심.
significance.en: Codifies the butterfly = stained-glass equation. Visual core of the reliquary period.

works.title: Lost Love
works.medium: glass, steel, water, fish, gynecological instruments, jewelry

exhibition_note.ko: 여성 산부인과 진료실을 수족관화. 살아있는 물고기와 잠긴 보석·의료기구.
exhibition_note.en: A gynecological consultation room aquariumized. Live fish, submerged jewelry, medical instruments.

significance.ko: FAITH·MORTALITY가 임상실로 응축. 여성 신체와 의학 권력의 진열.
significance.en: FAITH and MORTALITY condense in the clinic. Display of the female body and medical power.

works.title: Romance in the Age of Uncertainty

exhibition_note.ko: White Cube. 12사도+예수에 대응하는 12 진열장+중앙 십자가 구조.
exhibition_note.en: White Cube. 12 vitrines plus a central cross — corresponding to the 12 Apostles plus Christ.

significance.ko: Stations of the Cross의 진열장 번역. 신앙기의 구조적 정점.
significance.en: Vitrine translation of the Stations of the Cross. Structural apex of the reliquary period.

works.title: God Alone Knows
works.medium: stainless steel, glass, formaldehyde, three sheep crucified

exhibition_note.ko: 세 마리 양이 십자가 자세로 포름알데히드 안에. 골고다 도상의 박물학화.
exhibition_note.en: Three sheep in crucifixion postures within formaldehyde. The Golgotha iconography naturalized.

significance.ko: 기독교적 도살의 가시화. Away from the Flock의 신학적 확장.
significance.en: Christian sacrifice made visible. Theological expansion of Away from the Flock.

exhibition_note.ko: 약장의 신학적 표제. 약은 천국으로 가는 길의 지연.
exhibition_note.en: Theologically titled cabinet. Medication as the deferral of heaven.

significance.ko: 약학기와 신앙기의 봉합. 약장이 영적 어휘로 진입.
significance.en: Suture between pharmaceutical and reliquary periods. Cabinet enters spiritual vocabulary.

exhibition_note.ko: 임신한 여성을 절반은 정상, 절반은 해부도로 제시. 록펠러 센터 인근에 설치.
exhibition_note.en: Pregnant woman shown half-normal, half-anatomically flayed. Installed near Rockefeller Center.

significance.ko: 공공조각에서 모성 도상학을 해부학과 합체. 가톨릭 성모 도상의 21c 응답.
significance.en: Public sculpture fusing maternal iconography with anatomy. 21c response to the Catholic Madonna.

works.title: New Religion
works.collection: Multiple editions; All Hallows on the Wall (original install)

exhibition_note.ko: All Hallows on the Wall 교회 내부 설치. 박스 세트 형식.
exhibition_note.en: Installed inside All Hallows on the Wall church. Boxed-set format.

significance.ko: 신앙기의 명시적 표제. 약학적 신앙의 박스화·가정화.
significance.en: Explicitly titled marker of the reliquary period. Pharmaceutical faith boxed and domesticated.

exhibition_note.ko: Bernini 양식의 천사상에 해부학적 절단면을 노출.
exhibition_note.en: A Bernini-style angel with anatomically flayed sections exposed.

significance.ko: 고전 조각 + 해부학. 신성과 의학의 시각적 통합.
significance.en: Classical sculpture meets anatomy. Visual integration of the sacred and the medical.

works.title: The Tranquility of Solitude (For George Dyer)
works.medium: glass, steel, formaldehyde, three sheep, syringes, toilet

exhibition_note.ko: Francis Bacon의 연인 George Dyer 헌정. Bacon 회화 도상의 진열장 번역.
exhibition_note.en: Dedicated to Francis Bacon's lover George Dyer. Vitrine translation of Bacon's iconography.

significance.ko: Bacon 계보의 명시적 호명. 영국 회화 전통과 자연사 시리즈의 접합.
significance.en: Explicit invocation of the Bacon lineage. Junction of British painting tradition and the Natural History series.

works.title: For the Love of God
works.collection: Consortium including Hirst, Frank Dunphy, White Cube

exhibition_note.ko: White Cube 'Beyond Belief'. 18c 인간 두개골 캐스트 + 무결점 다이아 8,601개. 제작비 £14M.
exhibition_note.en: White Cube "Beyond Belief." Cast of an 18c human skull + 8,601 flawless diamonds. £14M production cost.

auction_record.note: £50M consortium sale; Hirst retains stake — pricing-as-event

significance.ko: 21c 바니타스의 정의적 작품. 자본주의적 메멘토 모리의 문자적 실현.
significance.en: Defining work of 21c vanitas. Literal realization of capitalist memento mori.

works.title: The Black Sheep with Golden Horn
works.medium: glass, steel, formaldehyde, sheep, gold-plated horns

exhibition_note.ko: 검은 양에 금 도금 뿔. 다이아 해골 시기의 자연사 변종.
exhibition_note.en: A black sheep with gold-plated horns. A natural-history variant from the diamond skull period.

significance.ko: VANITAS 어휘의 자연사 확장. 금=신성=시장의 등식.
significance.en: Vanitas vocabulary extended into natural history. Gold = sanctity = market.

works.title: The Golden Calf
works.medium: glass, gold-plated steel, formaldehyde, bullock with gold disk halo, gold hooves

exhibition_note.ko: Sotheby's <Beautiful Inside My Head Forever> 표제 출품작. 출애굽기 32장의 우상.
exhibition_note.en: Headline lot of Sotheby's Beautiful Inside My Head Forever. Idol from Exodus 32.

auction_record.note: Sotheby's Beautiful Inside My Head Forever, 2008.9.15 — Lehman day

significance.ko: Sotheby's 경매 = 작품 등식의 가장 직접적 도상. 우상숭배의 자기지칭.
significance.en: Most direct iconographic embodiment of the auction-as-artwork equation. Self-referential idolatry.

exhibition_note.ko: 1991 Shark의 17년 후 재제. Sotheby's 2008 출품 — 자기 도상의 경매화.
exhibition_note.en: Restaging of the 1991 Shark seventeen years on. Sold at Sotheby's 2008 — auctioning of one's own iconography.

significance.ko: 원형 도상의 시장 환매. 자기복제 = 자기시장화.
significance.en: Market repurchase of one's archetypal iconography. Self-replication = self-marketization.

works.title: Beautiful Inside My Head Forever (Sotheby's, Sept 15–16)

exhibition_note.ko: 리먼 파산 당일 시작된 이틀 경매. £111.5M, 단일작가 사상 최대 — 갤러리 우회 직판.
exhibition_note.en: Two-day auction beginning on the day Lehman fell. £111.5M, the largest single-artist sale ever — bypassing the galleries.

auction_record.note: 218 lots over Sept 15–16, 2008; coincides with Lehman collapse

significance.ko: 21c 미술시장사의 분기점. 경매 행위 자체가 단일 작품의 위상.
significance.en: Turning point of 21c art-market history. The auction act itself attains the status of a single work.

works.title: Fragments of Paradise
works.medium: stainless steel, glass, manufactured diamonds, mirror

exhibition_note.ko: 거울이 끼워진 약장. 자기반영적 진열의 등장.
exhibition_note.en: Mirrored cabinet. Emergence of the self-reflexive display.

significance.ko: 약장 + 거울 + 다이아. 자기 도상의 자기 진열.
significance.en: Cabinet + mirror + diamond. Self-iconography on self-display.

works.title: No Love Lost, Blue Paintings (Wallace Collection)

exhibition_note.ko: Wallace Collection 회화전. 25년 만의 직접 회화. 비평계 거의 만장 혹평.
exhibition_note.en: Wallace Collection painting show. Direct hand-painting after 25 years. Near-universal critical pan.

significance.ko: 위임 저자성에서 손으로의 첫 회귀. 비평적 실패가 차후 Veils의 자양분.
significance.en: First return from delegated authorship to the hand. The critical failure becomes nutrient for the later Veils.

works.title: End of an Era
works.medium: glass, steel, formaldehyde, severed bull's head with golden horns and crown

exhibition_note.ko: 황소 머리에 황금 뿔과 왕관. Vanitas Spectacle 시기의 종결.
exhibition_note.en: Bull's head with gold horns and crown. Closing chord of the Vanitas Spectacle period.

significance.ko: 스펙터클기의 자기 비문. 제목이 시기의 종결을 명시.
significance.en: Self-epitaph of the spectacle period. Title explicitly names the era's end.

works.title: Spot Painting (Gagosian Worldwide)
works.medium: household gloss on canvas (representative of 11-gallery global show)

exhibition_note.ko: Gagosian 전세계 11개 갤러리 동시 Spot 회고전(2012). 산업화의 정점이자 자기-기념.
exhibition_note.en: Gagosian's simultaneous Spot retrospective across 11 galleries (2012). Apex of industrialization, and self-monumentalization.

significance.ko: Spot의 글로벌 동시 진열 — 전시 자체가 작품. Mythopoeia 시기의 시장적 형식.
significance.en: Spots simultaneously displayed worldwide — the exhibition itself is the work. Market form of the Mythopoeia period.

exhibition_note.ko: Tate Modern 첫 개인 회고전(2012). 정전화의 공식 신호.
exhibition_note.en: First solo retrospective at Tate Modern (2012). Official signal of canonization.

significance.ko: 제도적 정전화. Mythopoeia 시기의 박물관적 보증.
significance.en: Institutional canonization. Museum warrant of the Mythopoeia period.

works.title: Black Sun
works.medium: dead flies on canvas with resin

exhibition_note.ko: 수만 마리 죽은 파리를 캔버스에 고정. 검은 단색 회화의 곤충학적 번역.
exhibition_note.en: Tens of thousands of dead flies fixed on canvas. Entomological translation of black monochrome painting.

significance.ko: 단색 회화의 자연사화. Reinhardt·Malevich의 검정에 부패를 주입.
significance.en: Naturalization of monochrome painting. Decay injected into the black of Reinhardt and Malevich.

exhibition_note.ko: 유럽 주요 성당의 장미창을 나비 도상으로 번역한 판화 시리즈.
exhibition_note.en: Print series translating major European cathedral rose windows into butterfly iconography.

significance.ko: 신앙기 모티프의 판화-에디션화. 시장 접근성과 신성성의 결합.
significance.en: Print-editioning of reliquary motifs. Market accessibility meets sanctity.

works.title: Gone but Not Forgotten
works.medium: gold-plated woolly mammoth skeleton in glass tank with gold-painted formaldehyde

exhibition_note.ko: 도금된 매머드 골격을 금색 포름알데히드 안에. 멸종과 황금의 결합.
exhibition_note.en: Gilded mammoth skeleton in gold-tinted formaldehyde. Extinction conjoined with gold.

significance.ko: vitrine 형식의 만년 변종. 멸종 = 궁극의 vanitas.
significance.en: Late variant of the vitrine form. Extinction = ultimate vanitas.

works.title: Demon with Bowl
works.medium: painted resin, 18.3m height (Punta della Dogana atrium)

exhibition_note.ko: Treasures from the Wreck of the Unbelievable의 입구 작품. Punta della Dogana 중앙 홀.
exhibition_note.en: Entry work of Treasures from the Wreck of the Unbelievable. Central hall of Punta della Dogana.

significance.ko: 위서적 신화 제조의 도입부. 18m 스케일이 진위 여부 자체를 무력화.
significance.en: Overture to fictional mythopoeia. The 18m scale neutralizes the question of authenticity itself.

exhibition_note.ko: Treasures 핵심 작품. 산호와 공작석으로 위장된 가짜 해저 유물.
exhibition_note.en: Key Treasures work. Fictional seabed artifact disguised in coral and malachite.

significance.ko: 허구의 진본성. 신화·고고학·자본의 삼중 위조.
significance.en: Fictional authenticity. A triple forgery of myth, archaeology, and capital.

exhibition_note.ko: Die Antwoord 멤버 Yo-Landi 얼굴이 합성된 가짜 고대 신상. 시대착오의 의도적 노출.
exhibition_note.en: Faux-antique deity blended with the face of Die Antwoord's Yo-Landi. Anachronism deliberately exposed.

significance.ko: Treasures 위서성의 자기-폭로 장치. 동시대 도상이 가짜 고대 안에 매장됨.
significance.en: Self-disclosing device of Treasures' fictionality. A contemporary face buried in faux-antiquity.

exhibition_note.ko: 그리스 히드라와 힌두 칼리의 결투. 비교종교적 도상의 충돌 무대.
exhibition_note.en: Greek Hydra against Hindu Kali. Stage of clashing comparative-religious iconography.

significance.ko: 단일 신화 체계의 거부. 종교 도상학 간 합성 신화 제조.
significance.en: Refusal of any single mythic system. Synthetic myth-making across iconographies.

exhibition_note.ko: Gagosian LA 'The Veil Paintings'(2018). Veils 시리즈의 표제급.
exhibition_note.en: Gagosian LA "The Veil Paintings" (2018). Title-level work of the Veils series.

significance.ko: 위임 저자성에서 손으로의 본격 회귀. Bonnard·Seurat 후계.
significance.en: Full-scale return from delegated authorship to the hand. Successor to Bonnard and Seurat.

exhibition_note.ko: Veil 시리즈의 핵심 회화 중 하나. 색-입자의 미세 진동.
exhibition_note.en: Among the core paintings of the Veil series. Micro-vibration of color particles.

significance.ko: 회화로의 복귀가 'Faith·Love' 같은 추상명사 표제로 신앙기와 연속성을 확보.
significance.en: Return to painting secures continuity with the reliquary period through abstract-noun titles like Faith and Love.

exhibition_note.ko: Cherry Blossoms 시리즈 첫 해 작업. 이후 3년간 107점으로 확장.
exhibition_note.en: First-year work of the Cherry Blossoms series. Expands to 107 paintings over the next three years.

significance.ko: 벚꽃 시리즈의 출발. mono no aware의 명시적 차용.
significance.en: Genesis of the Cherry Blossoms series. Explicit citation of mono no aware.

works.title: Mandala — Reverence
works.medium: butterflies and household gloss on canvas

exhibition_note.ko: Mandala Paintings 첫 시리즈. 나비 날개로 그린 불교 우주도.
exhibition_note.en: First Mandala Paintings series. Buddhist cosmograms made of butterfly wings.

significance.ko: 신앙 어휘의 비가톨릭화. Kaleidoscope의 동방 확장.
significance.en: Reliquary vocabulary moves beyond Catholicism. Eastern expansion of the Kaleidoscope.

works.title: Empresses (series, butterfly-wing on canvas)
works.medium: butterflies and household gloss on canvas

exhibition_note.ko: 비잔틴 이콘의 여성 통치자 도상을 나비로 번역.
exhibition_note.en: Translation of Byzantine icons of female rulers into butterfly form.

significance.ko: 이콘과 통치권의 도상. 여성 권력의 종교적 시각화.
significance.en: Iconography of icon and sovereignty. Religious visualization of female power.

exhibition_note.ko: Cherry Blossoms 후기. Fondation Cartier(파리, 2021) 출품작군 중 하나.
exhibition_note.en: Late Cherry Blossoms. Among the works exhibited at Fondation Cartier (Paris, 2021).

significance.ko: 벚꽃 시리즈의 정점. 임파스토 신체성과 색-시간의 응축.
significance.en: Apex of the Cherry Blossoms series. Condensation of impasto bodily presence and color-time.

works.title: Where Will I Find Such Joys? (Cherry Blossom)

exhibition_note.ko: Cartier 전 동시기 작업. 무상에 대한 정조가 표제로 직진.
exhibition_note.en: Coterminous with the Cartier show. Title moves directly toward the pathos of impermanence.

significance.ko: 표제가 mono no aware 정조를 직역. 후기 회화의 감정 좌표.
significance.en: Title literalizes the mono no aware sentiment. Affective coordinate of the late paintings.

exhibition_note.ko: Fondation Cartier 파리(2021.7.6–2022.1.2). 107 점 시리즈 중 30+점 공개.
exhibition_note.en: Fondation Cartier Paris (Jul 6 2021 – Jan 2 2022). 30+ of the 107-piece series shown.

significance.ko: Hirst 회화 시기의 제도적 정점. 손의 회복이 박물관에 봉헌됨.
significance.en: Institutional apex of Hirst's painting period. The recovery of the hand is consecrated in the museum.

works.title: The Currency
works.medium: 10,000 enamel paintings on paper, paired with 10,000 NFTs

exhibition_note.ko: 각 소유자가 1년 내 회화 또는 NFT 중 하나 선택. 미선택분은 소각/소멸.
exhibition_note.en: Each owner chooses within one year: painting or NFT. The unchosen is destroyed/annihilated.

significance.ko: 물질·디지털 가치의 양자택일을 작품 형식에 봉인. 2008 Sotheby's의 디지털 응답.
significance.en: Seals the material/digital choice into the work's very form. Digital response to Sotheby's 2008.

works.title: The Currency Burning Ceremony
works.medium: performance: incineration of 4,851 paintings, Newport Street Gallery

exhibition_note.ko: 디지털을 선택한 소유자들의 회화 4,851점을 Hirst 본인이 소각(2022.10).
exhibition_note.en: Hirst himself incinerates 4,851 paintings whose owners chose the digital (October 2022).

significance.ko: 물리적 파괴가 곧 가치 실험의 종결. 가치=선택의 도상학.
significance.en: Physical destruction as the terminus of the value experiment. Iconography of value-as-choice.

exhibition_note.ko: Gagosian Paris/NY 'Cherry Blossoms'(2022) 출품. 후기 벚꽃의 두꺼워진 임파스토.
exhibition_note.en: Exhibited at Gagosian Paris/NY "Cherry Blossoms" (2022). Thicker impasto of the late blossoms.

significance.ko: 벚꽃 시리즈의 시장적 전개. 표제는 트라우마 사후 어휘.
significance.en: Market unfolding of the Cherry Blossoms series. Title borrows post-traumatic vocabulary.

exhibition_note.ko: Sacred Heart 회화 시리즈. 가톨릭 성심 도상의 명시적 회귀.
exhibition_note.en: Sacred Heart painting series. Explicit return of the Catholic Sacred Heart iconography.

significance.ko: 가톨릭 도상학의 표제급 명시 회귀. 후기 신앙 어휘의 대표.
significance.en: Title-level explicit return of Catholic iconography. Emblem of the late reliquary vocabulary.

works.title: The Beauty of Death (Mandala)
works.medium: butterflies and household gloss on canvas

exhibition_note.ko: Mandala 시리즈 후기. 표제가 바니타스와 신성기하학을 봉합.
exhibition_note.en: Late Mandala series. Title sutures vanitas to sacred geometry.

significance.ko: MORTALITY와 FAITH의 봉합. 죽음의 아름다움이 도상학적 양립.
significance.en: Suture of MORTALITY and FAITH. Death's beauty made iconographically compatible.

works.title: Where the Land Meets the Sea (Seascape)

exhibition_note.ko: Seascape 회화 시리즈. Veils 점묘 기법을 풍경에 적용.
exhibition_note.en: Seascape painting series. Veils' pointillist technique applied to landscape.

significance.ko: 회화기의 풍경화 확장. 점묘=색입자=공기의 등식.
significance.en: Landscape extension of the painting period. The pointillism = color particle = air equation.

works.title: The Empresses (late)
works.medium: butterflies and gold leaf on canvas

exhibition_note.ko: Empresses 시리즈 후기, 금박 도입. Gagosian 전.
exhibition_note.en: Late Empresses series with gold leaf. Gagosian show.

significance.ko: 비잔틴 이콘의 금박 직접 차용. 신성성 = 금속 = 시장의 삼중 결합.
significance.en: Direct citation of Byzantine icon's gold leaf. Triple binding of sanctity, metal, and market.

works.title: Reverence (Mandala, late)
works.medium: butterflies and household gloss on canvas

exhibition_note.ko: Mandala 후기 단색조. 신성기하학의 미세 채도 변화.
exhibition_note.en: Late monochromatic Mandala. Subtle chromatic shift in sacred geometry.

significance.ko: 신성기하학의 단색화. Reinhardt 검정의 동방적 응답.
significance.en: Monochromatization of sacred geometry. Eastern response to Reinhardt's black.

exhibition_note.ko: 최근 진열장 작업. 30년 전 형식의 현대적 재현.
exhibition_note.en: Recent vitrine work. Contemporary restaging of the 30-year-old form.

significance.ko: 자연사 시리즈의 만년 잔존. 회화기 한가운데에서의 Mortality 축의 재호명.
significance.en: Late survival of the Natural History series. Re-invocation of the Mortality axis amid the painting period.

works.title: Looking Forward to a Complete Suspension of Disbelief (Veils, late)

exhibition_note.ko: Veils 만년작. 표제가 Coleridge의 미학적 명제 인용.
exhibition_note.en: Late Veil. Title cites Coleridge's aesthetic proposition.

significance.ko: 회화의 미학적 자기-언명. 'suspension of disbelief'은 Treasures의 회고적 정당화.
significance.en: Aesthetic self-statement of painting. "Suspension of disbelief" retroactively justifies Treasures.

exhibition_note.ko: 약물명 Spot의 박물관 정전화. 시리즈가 미국 주요 박물관에 진입.
exhibition_note.en: Museum canonization of the drug-named Spot. Series enters major US museums.

significance.ko: Spot 시리즈의 박물관 입성. 산업적 격자의 학예적 보증.
significance.en: Spot series enters the museum. Curatorial warrant of the industrial grid.

exhibition_note.ko: Shark 도상의 신앙기 변종. 표제가 부정 신학을 명시.
exhibition_note.en: Reliquary-period variant of the Shark iconography. Title explicitly invokes negative theology.

significance.ko: 1991 Shark의 신학적 응답. 죽음 부정의 도상학적 표면화.
significance.en: Theological response to the 1991 Shark. The denial of death surfaced as iconography.

works.title: Beautiful, Cyclonic, Sublime, Bleeding, Slashing, Hurricane Painting

exhibition_note.ko: Spin 명명 규칙의 극단적 적용. 형용사 누적이 작품 메타데이터.
exhibition_note.en: Extreme application of the Spin naming convention. Adjective accretion is the work's metadata.

significance.ko: 표제 자체가 작품. 명명=태깅의 자기지시.
significance.en: Title is itself the work. Self-reference of naming-as-tagging.
